seoTitle: Dapu Timing Products Distributor | Oscillators, TCXO, RF Filters | LiTong
seoDescription: Authorized Dapu Microelectronics distributor. Supplying crystal oscillators, TCXOs, VCXOs, RF filters, and sensors. Get selection guide and technical support.

faqs.question: What timing products does Dapu offer?
faqs.answer: Dapu offers a comprehensive portfolio of timing products including crystal oscillators (XO) for general-purpose timing, temperature-compensated oscillators (TCXO) for high-stability applications, voltage-controlled oscillators (VCXO) for PLL and frequency synthesis, oven-controlled oscillators (OCXO) for ultra-high stability, and clock generators for system timing. Products cover frequency ranges from kHz to GHz with various package options including standard DIP, SMD, and small-form-factor ceramic packages. Dapu also offers custom frequency programming services for specific application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Review the product categories based on your stability, jitter, and frequency requirements. Contact our FAE team for detailed selection guidance.

faqs.question: How do I select the right oscillator for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right oscillator depends on several key parameters. First, determine your frequency stability requirement - standard XOs offer ±20-50ppm, TCXOs provide ±0.5-2.5ppm, and OCXOs achieve ±0.01-0.1ppm. Second, evaluate jitter requirements - applications like Ethernet and SERDES need sub-1ps jitter, while general microprocessor clocks can tolerate higher jitter. Third, consider operating temperature range - commercial (0-70°C), industrial (-40-85°C), or automotive (-40-125°C). Fourth, assess power consumption constraints - TCXOs consume more power than standard XOs. Fifth, determine package size requirements - smaller packages cost more but save board space. Our FAE team can help analyze your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Define your key parameters (stability, jitter, temperature, power) and contact our FAE team for personalized recommendations.

faqs.question: What RF products are available from Dapu?
faqs.answer: Dapu offers a range of RF components for wireless communication applications. RF filters include SAW filters, BAW filters, and ceramic filters for various frequency bands including cellular (700MHz-2.7GHz), WiFi (2.4GHz/5GHz), Bluetooth, and ISM bands. Low noise amplifiers (LNAs) provide high gain with low noise figure for receiver front-ends. RF switches offer high isolation for antenna switching and filter banks. Power amplifiers are available for low-power transmitter applications. These components support 5G, 4G LTE, WiFi 6, Bluetooth 5, and other wireless standards. Dapu can also provide custom RF solutions for specific frequency bands and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Identify your frequency band and performance requirements. Contact us for RF component recommendations and custom solutions.

faqs.question: What is the difference between XO, TCXO, and VCXO?
faqs.answer: Crystal Oscillators (XO) are the basic type using a quartz crystal to generate a stable frequency. They offer good stability (±20-50ppm) at low cost and are suitable for general-purpose clocking. Temperature-Compensated Crystal Oscillators (TCXO) add temperature compensation circuitry to achieve much better stability (±0.5-2.5ppm) across temperature variations. They're ideal for GPS, cellular, and wireless applications. Voltage-Controlled Crystal Oscillators (VCXO) allow frequency adjustment through a control voltage, making them suitable for PLL applications and frequency synchronization. They typically offer stability between XO and TCXO. The choice depends on your stability requirements, temperature range, and whether you need frequency adjustment capability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use XO for general timing, TCXO for high-stability applications, VCXO for PLL and synchronization. Contact us for specific recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the price range for Dapu timing products?
faqs.answer: Dapu timing products are competitively priced compared to international brands. Standard crystal oscillators range from $0.10-$0.50 depending on frequency and package. TCXOs typically cost $0.50-$3.00 based on stability grade and temperature range. VCXOs range from $0.80-$4.00 depending on pull range and linearity. OCXOs for high-stability applications range from $20-$100+. RF filters range from $0.05-$0.30 for standard bands. LNAs range from $0.20-$1.00 depending on frequency and performance. Custom frequency parts may have setup charges. Volume discounts are available for high-quantity orders. As an authorized distributor, we offer competitive pricing and can provide quotations based on your specific requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current pricing and volume discounts. We can provide competitive quotations for your production requirements.

categories.name: Crystal Oscillators (XO)
categories.description: High-precision crystal oscillators offering low jitter, wide frequency range, and various package options for general-purpose timing applications. Dapu XO products provide reliable clock sources for microprocessors, communication interfaces, and consumer electronics.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select Crystal Oscillators
selectionGuide.description: Consider frequency accuracy, jitter requirements, and package size when selecting crystal oscillators.

faqs.question: What frequency range do Dapu crystal oscillators support?
faqs.answer: Dapu crystal oscillators support a wide frequency range from 1MHz to 200MHz for standard products. Low-frequency oscillators (32.768kHz) are available for real-time clock applications. High-frequency options up to 1.5GHz are available in PLL-based clock generators. The most common frequencies include 8MHz, 12MHz, 16MHz, 24MHz, 25MHz, 48MHz, and 50MHz for microcontroller and processor applications. Communication frequencies include 25MHz and 125MHz for Ethernet, 26MHz and 38.4MHz for cellular, and various frequencies for video and display applications. Custom frequencies can be programmed for volume orders.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select standard frequencies for cost savings, custom frequencies for specific requirements. Contact us for custom frequency availability.

faqs.question: What package options are available for Dapu oscillators?
faqs.answer: Dapu oscillators are available in various package options to meet different application requirements. Standard SMD packages include 2.0x1.6mm, 2.5x2.0mm, 3.2x2.5mm, 5.0x3.2mm, and 7.0x5.0mm ceramic packages. Smaller packages (1.6x1.2mm) are available for space-constrained applications. DIP packages (Half-size, Full-size) are available for through-hole mounting. Specialized packages include ceramic SMD with metal lids for better EMI performance. Automotive-grade packages meet AEC-Q200 requirements. The package selection affects cost, board space, and thermal characteristics. Smaller packages typically cost more but save PCB area.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose package based on board space constraints and cost budget. Standard 3.2x2.5mm offers good balance of size and cost.

faqs.question: What is the typical jitter performance of Dapu crystal oscillators?
faqs.answer: Dapu crystal oscillators offer competitive jitter performance suitable for most applications. Standard XOs typically achieve 1-3ps RMS phase jitter (12kHz-20MHz integration range), adequate for general-purpose clocking. Low-jitter versions achieve <1ps RMS for high-speed SERDES and communication applications. Period jitter is typically <100ps peak-to-peak. The jitter performance depends on crystal quality, oscillator circuit design, and power supply noise. For applications requiring ultra-low jitter (<0.5ps), consider Dapu's premium low-jitter series or clock generators with integrated jitter attenuation. Phase noise plots are available for critical applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select standard XO for <3ps jitter requirements, low-jitter series for <1ps requirements. Contact us for phase noise data.

faqs.question: How do I calculate load capacitance for crystal oscillators?
faqs.answer: Load capacitance (CL) is critical for crystal oscillator frequency accuracy. The total load capacitance seen by the crystal is calculated as: CL = (C1 * C2) / (C1 + C2) + Cs, where C1 and C2 are the external load capacitors, and Cs is the stray capacitance (typically 3-5pF). For example, if CL specification is 20pF and Cs is 4pF, then (C1 * C2) / (C1 + C2) = 16pF. If C1 = C2, then each capacitor should be 32pF. Dapu oscillators specify the required load capacitance in the datasheet. Using incorrect load capacitance causes frequency offset. Most Dapu oscillators have built-in load capacitors and don't require external calculation - check the datasheet for specific part numbers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use Dapu oscillators with built-in load caps to simplify design. For discrete crystals, follow the calculation formula above.

faqs.question: Can Dapu oscillators drive multiple loads?
faqs.answer: Dapu oscillators can drive multiple loads depending on output type and fanout capability. CMOS output oscillators can typically drive 10-15 standard CMOS loads (15pF each). For driving multiple loads, use a clock buffer or place loads close to the oscillator to minimize trace capacitance. LVCMOS outputs provide better drive capability than clipped sine outputs. For high fanout applications (>20 loads), use a dedicated clock distribution chip or zero-delay buffer. Differential outputs (LVDS, LVPECL) can drive multiple loads through proper termination. When driving multiple loads, ensure total capacitance doesn't exceed the oscillator's drive capability as specified in the datasheet.
faqs.decisionGuide: For multiple loads, use clock buffers or place loads close to oscillator. Contact us for high fanout solutions.

products.name: 25MHz Crystal Oscillator 3.2x2.5mm
products.shortDescription: Standard 25MHz crystal oscillator in 3.2x2.5mm ceramic package with ±30ppm stability and 3.3V operation for...
descriptionParagraphs: The XO3225-25M is a high-quality 25MHz crystal oscillator designed for general-purpose timing applications in a compact 3.2x2.5mm ceramic package.

Featuring ±30ppm frequency stability over commercial temperature range, 3.3V operation, and CMOS output, this oscillator provides reliable clocking for microprocessors, Ethernet controllers, and communication interfaces.
With low power consumption, fast startup time, and excellent reliability, the XO3225-25M is ideal for cost-sensitive applications requiring stable timing.
features: Standard 25MHz frequency for Ethernet and processor applications

faeReview.author: David Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Timing Products
faeReview.content: The XO3225-25M is one of our most popular oscillators due to its versatility and cost-effectiveness. In my experience supporting customer designs, this part works reliably in a wide range of applications from Ethernet switches to industrial controllers. The 25MHz frequency is the standard reference for 100Mbps Ethernet PHYs, making this oscillator essential for network equipment. I particularly appreciate the consistent quality and short lead times. The 3.2x2.5mm package is a good balance between board space and ease of handling. For customers designing Ethernet equipment, I often recommend this part as the primary clock source. The ±30ppm stability is adequate for most non-telecom applications.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent cost-performance ratio for Ethernet and general-purpose timing applications.

alternativeParts.useCase: Compact designs where board space is critical

alternativeParts.reason: Lower jitter for high-speed SERDES applications
alternativeParts.useCase: Gigabit Ethernet and high-speed serial links

companionParts.description: Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: Is the XO3225-25M suitable for Gigabit Ethernet?
faqs.answer: The XO3225-25M is suitable for 10/100Mbps Ethernet applications where the 25MHz reference clock is used. For Gigabit Ethernet (1000Mbps), a 125MHz oscillator is typically required, or a 25MHz oscillator with PLL multiplication in the PHY. Some Gigabit PHYs can accept 25MHz input and multiply internally. Check your specific PHY datasheet for clock requirements. For Gigabit applications requiring low jitter, consider the XO3225-25M-LJ low-jitter version or a 125MHz oscillator. The standard XO3225-25M provides adequate performance for most 10/100Mbps Ethernet applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use for 10/100Mbps Ethernet. For Gigabit, verify PHY requirements or use 125MHz oscillator.

faqs.question: What is the startup time of the XO3225-25M?
faqs.answer: The XO3225-25M has a typical startup time of less than 5 milliseconds from power-on to stable output. Maximum startup time is 10ms over all conditions. Startup time is measured from when VDD reaches 90% of nominal voltage to when output frequency stabilizes within ±10ppm of final value. The fast startup makes this oscillator suitable for applications requiring quick power-up sequences. Startup time may be slightly longer at cold temperatures (-40°C). For applications requiring even faster startup, Dapu offers fast-start versions with <2ms startup time. The startup time specification ensures reliable operation in power-managed systems with frequent on/off cycles.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard version suitable for most applications. Fast-start version available for power-managed systems.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for XO3225-25M?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of XO3225-25M?
faqs.answer: The XO3225-25M has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for high-reliability applications. Contact us for detailed reliability reports.

faqs.question: What is the ESD sensitivity rating of XO3225-25M?
faqs.answer: This component has an ESD HBM (Human Body Model) rating of 2kV and CDM (Charged Device Model) rating of 500V. While this provides reasonable protection, standard ESD precautions should always be followed during handling and assembly. Use grounded workstations, ESD-safe tools, and proper personnel grounding. Avoid touching pins directly. Store components in ESD-safe packaging until ready for use. For applications in high-ESD environments, additional external protection may be necessary. Contact our FAE team for ESD design recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard ESD precautions. Contact us for high-ESD environment recommendations.

products.name: 125MHz Crystal Oscillator for Gigabit Ethernet
products.shortDescription: 125MHz low-jitter crystal oscillator in 3.2x2.5mm package with ±25ppm stability for Gigabit Ethernet and high-speed...
descriptionParagraphs: The XO3225-125M is a high-performance 125MHz crystal oscillator specifically designed for Gigabit Ethernet and high-speed communication applications.
Featuring low phase jitter (<1ps RMS), ±25ppm stability, and 3.3V CMOS output, this oscillator meets the stringent timing requirements of Gigabit Ethernet PHYs and SERDES applications.
The 3.2x2.5mm ceramic package provides excellent reliability while maintaining compact size for high-density designs.
features: 3.3V CMOS output with fast rise/fall times

faeReview.author: Michael Zhang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Communication Timing
faeReview.content: The XO3225-125M is my go-to recommendation for Gigabit Ethernet applications. The <1ps jitter specification meets IEEE 802.3 requirements for 1000BASE-T, and I've successfully used it in numerous switch and router designs. The industrial temperature range is valuable for networking equipment that may operate in uncontrolled environments. What sets this part apart is the consistent jitter performance across temperature - some competitors show significant jitter degradation at temperature extremes. The 3.2x2.5mm package is compatible with most Gigabit PHY reference designs. For customers designing enterprise networking equipment, this oscillator provides the right balance of performance, reliability, and cost.
faeReview.highlight: Low jitter and wide temperature range ideal for Gigabit Ethernet applications.

alternativeParts.reason: Larger package for easier handling and better thermal performance
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications where larger package is acceptable for easier assembly

companionParts.description: Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: What jitter specification is required for Gigabit Ethernet?
faqs.answer: Gigabit Ethernet (1000BASE-T) requires specific jitter performance to ensure reliable data transmission. The IEEE 802.3 standard specifies maximum peak-to-peak jitter of 1.4ns at the transmitter. For the reference clock, typical requirements are <1ps RMS phase jitter (integrated 12kHz-20MHz). The XO3225-125M with <1ps RMS jitter meets these requirements with margin. Some Gigabit PHYs have more stringent requirements, especially for SGMII or RGMII interfaces. Always check your specific PHY datasheet for exact clock requirements. Dapu can provide phase noise plots and jitter measurements for qualification. For 10 Gigabit Ethernet, even lower jitter (<0.5ps) is typically required.
faqs.decisionGuide: XO3225-125M meets standard Gigabit Ethernet requirements. Verify specific PHY datasheet for exact specs.

faqs.question: Can the XO3225-125M be used for other frequencies?
faqs.answer: The XO3225-125M is factory-programmed to 125MHz and cannot be changed in the field. However, Dapu offers the same oscillator series in various standard frequencies including 100MHz, 106.25MHz, 125MHz, 156.25MHz, and 200MHz for different applications. Custom frequencies can be programmed at the factory for volume orders. If you need multiple frequencies in your design, consider using a programmable clock generator or oscillator with multiple output options. The XO3225 series uses the same package and pinout across frequencies, allowing for easy substitution during design changes. Contact us for the complete frequency availability list.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select exact frequency needed. Custom frequencies available for volume orders.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for XO3225-125M?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of XO3225-125M?
faqs.answer: The XO3225-125M has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for high-reliability applications. Contact us for detailed reliability reports.

faqs.question: What is the ESD sensitivity rating of XO3225-125M?
faqs.answer: This component has an ESD HBM (Human Body Model) rating of 2kV and CDM (Charged Device Model) rating of 500V. While this provides reasonable protection, standard ESD precautions should always be followed during handling and assembly. Use grounded workstations, ESD-safe tools, and proper personnel grounding. Avoid touching pins directly. Store components in ESD-safe packaging until ready for use. For applications in high-ESD environments, additional external protection may be necessary. Contact our FAE team for ESD design recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard ESD precautions. Contact us for high-ESD environment recommendations.

categories.longDescription: Dapu Crystal Oscillators (XO) provide reliable timing solutions for a wide range of electronic applications. These high-precision oscillators offer excellent frequency stability, low jitter, and various package options to meet diverse design requirements. As an authorized Dapu distributor, we offer comprehensive selection guidance and technical support for crystal oscillator applications including microprocessor clocks, communication interfaces, and consumer electronics. Dapu XO products feature competitive performance, short lead times, and cost-effective pricing compared to international brands.

series.name: XO3225 Series
series.description: 3.2x2.5mm standard crystal oscillators for general-purpose timing

series.name: XO2520 Series
series.description: 2.5x2.0mm compact crystal oscillators for space-constrained designs

categories.name: Temperature Compensated Oscillators (TCXO)
categories.description: High-stability TCXOs with excellent frequency stability over temperature for GPS, cellular, and wireless communication applications. Dapu TCXOs provide temperature compensation achieving ±0.5ppm to ±2.5ppm stability.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select TCXOs
selectionGuide.description: Consider stability grade, temperature range, and phase noise requirements when selecting TCXOs.

faqs.question: What stability grades are available for Dapu TCXOs?
faqs.answer: Dapu TCXOs are available in multiple stability grades to meet different application requirements. Standard grades include ±2.5ppm for consumer and industrial applications, ±1.0ppm for wireless communication, ±0.5ppm for GPS and precision timing. The stability specification represents the maximum frequency deviation over the specified temperature range. Higher stability (lower ppm) comes at higher cost and power consumption. GPS applications typically require ±0.5ppm or better for fast lock and accurate positioning. Cellular applications usually need ±1.0-2.5ppm depending on the standard. Select the grade that meets your system requirements with appropriate margin.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select ±0.5ppm for GPS, ±1.0ppm for cellular, ±2.5ppm for general wireless. Contact us for specific recommendations.

faqs.question: How does temperature compensation work in TCXOs?
faqs.answer: TCXOs use temperature compensation circuits to counteract the natural frequency drift of quartz crystals with temperature. The crystal has a parabolic or S-shaped frequency vs. temperature curve with turning points around 25-30°C. A temperature sensor (thermistor or integrated sensor) measures the ambient temperature. Compensation circuitry generates a correction voltage based on the temperature reading. This voltage is applied to a varactor diode in the oscillator circuit, pulling the frequency in the opposite direction of the crystal's drift. The result is much flatter frequency vs. temperature characteristics, achieving 10-50x improvement over standard crystals. Dapu TCXOs use advanced compensation algorithms for optimal stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: TCXO compensation is automatic. Select stability grade based on your application requirements.

faqs.question: What is the hysteresis of Dapu TCXOs?
faqs.answer: Hysteresis in TCXOs refers to the frequency difference observed when approaching a temperature from opposite directions (heating vs cooling). Dapu TCXOs typically exhibit very low hysteresis of <0.1ppm, which is negligible for most applications. Low hysteresis is achieved through high-quality crystal material and optimized compensation algorithms. For precision applications where hysteresis could be a concern, we can provide characterization data. The low hysteresis ensures consistent frequency regardless of temperature history, which is important for applications with cyclic temperature variations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Low hysteresis <0.1ppm suitable for all applications. Contact us for characterization data if needed.

faqs.question: Can TCXOs be used in vacuum or space applications?
faqs.answer: Dapu TCXOs can be used in vacuum and space applications with proper consideration. In vacuum, heat transfer is different (no convection), which affects thermal characteristics. The TCXO may run slightly warmer in vacuum, but the temperature compensation still functions correctly. For space applications, radiation tolerance must be considered. Standard commercial TCXOs are not radiation-hardened. For space applications, we recommend using hermetically sealed packages and considering radiation effects. Contact our technical team for guidance on vacuum and space applications. Custom screening and testing can be arranged for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for vacuum with thermal considerations. Contact us for space application guidance.

faqs.question: What is the retrace specification of Dapu TCXOs?
faqs.answer: Retrace (or warm-up repeatability) is the frequency deviation observed when power is cycled. Dapu TCXOs typically have excellent retrace of <0.1ppm after the initial warm-up period. This means if you power off and then power on again after the oscillator has stabilized, it will return to nearly the same frequency. Good retrace is important for applications with frequent power cycling. The retrace specification assumes the TCXO has completed its initial warm-up before power-off. For applications requiring the absolute best retrace, allow the TCXO to stabilize for several minutes before relying on the frequency accuracy.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent retrace <0.1ppm. Allow full warm-up for best repeatability.

products.name: 26MHz TCXO ±0.5ppm GPS Grade
products.shortDescription: High-stability 26MHz TCXO in 3.2x2.5mm package with ±0.5ppm stability for GPS and precision timing applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The TCXO3225-26M-05 is a high-performance temperature-compensated crystal oscillator providing exceptional frequency stability for GPS and precision timing applications.
Featuring ±0.5ppm stability over -30°C to +85°C, 26MHz frequency, and low phase noise, this TCXO meets the stringent requirements of GPS receivers and cellular applications.
The compact 3.2x2.5mm package and 3.3V operation make it ideal for portable and battery-powered devices requiring accurate timing.
features: Low phase noise for fast GPS lock

faeReview.author: Lisa Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - RF Timing
faeReview.content: The TCXO3225-26M-05 is our premium TCXO for GPS applications, and it delivers excellent performance at a competitive price point. I've used this part in numerous GPS tracker and navigation device designs with excellent results. The ±0.5ppm stability ensures fast TTFF (Time To First Fix) and accurate positioning. What impresses me is the consistent performance across different production batches - important for high-volume manufacturing. The phase noise performance is comparable to much more expensive Japanese TCXOs. For customers designing GPS-enabled products, this TCXO provides the right balance of performance and cost. I recommend it for any GPS application requiring <1ppm stability.
faeReview.highlight: GPS-grade stability at competitive pricing for navigation and positioning applications.

alternativeParts.reason: Lower cost option for less stringent applications
Higher frequency for LTE cellular applications

companionParts.description: Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: Is this TCXO suitable for GPS applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, the TCXO3225-26M-05 with ±0.5ppm stability is specifically designed for GPS and GNSS applications. GPS receivers require high-stability timing references for accurate satellite signal acquisition and tracking. The ±0.5ppm stability ensures that the receiver can maintain lock on weak satellite signals and achieve accurate positioning. The 26MHz frequency is the standard reference frequency for most GPS chipsets. The low phase noise (-135dBc/Hz @ 1kHz) helps achieve fast TTFF (Time To First Fix). This TCXO has been successfully deployed in various GPS trackers, navigation devices, and location-based services. For high-precision GPS (RTK, surveying), consider OCXO products with even better stability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for standard GPS/GNSS applications. Contact us for high-precision GPS recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the power consumption of this TCXO?
faqs.answer: The TCXO3225-26M-05 consumes approximately 2-3mA typical at 3.3V, which is higher than standard crystal oscillators due to the temperature compensation circuitry. The power consumption is relatively constant across temperature. For battery-powered applications, this power consumption should be factored into the overall power budget. Some TCXOs have a standby mode that reduces power consumption when the oscillator is not needed. The higher power consumption is the trade-off for achieving excellent temperature stability. For ultra-low-power applications, consider using a standard XO with good temperature characteristics or implementing software compensation. Contact us for power-sensitive application recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Factor 2-3mA into power budget. Contact us for low-power timing alternatives if needed.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for TCXO3225-26M-05?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of TCXO3225-26M-05?
faqs.answer: The TCXO3225-26M-05 has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for high-reliability applications. Contact us for detailed reliability reports.

faqs.question: What is the ESD sensitivity rating of TCXO3225-26M-05?
faqs.answer: This component has an ESD HBM (Human Body Model) rating of 2kV and CDM (Charged Device Model) rating of 500V. While this provides reasonable protection, standard ESD precautions should always be followed during handling and assembly. Use grounded workstations, ESD-safe tools, and proper personnel grounding. Avoid touching pins directly. Store components in ESD-safe packaging until ready for use. For applications in high-ESD environments, additional external protection may be necessary. Contact our FAE team for ESD design recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard ESD precautions. Contact us for high-ESD environment recommendations.

products.name: 38.4MHz TCXO ±1.0ppm for LTE
products.shortDescription: 38.4MHz TCXO in 3.2x2.5mm package with ±1.0ppm stability for 4G LTE cellular and wireless applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The TCXO3225-38M4-10 is a high-quality temperature-compensated crystal oscillator optimized for 4G LTE cellular and wireless communication applications.
Featuring ±1.0ppm stability over -30°C to +85°C and 38.4MHz frequency, this TCXO provides reliable timing for LTE basebands, cellular modules, and wireless transceivers.
The compact 3.2x2.5mm package and excellent phase noise performance make it ideal for smartphones, tablets, and IoT devices.
features: Low phase noise for reliable communication

faeReview.author: Robert Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wireless Timing
faeReview.content: The TCXO3225-38M4-10 is our most popular TCXO for cellular applications. The 38.4MHz frequency is the standard reference for most 4G LTE chipsets, and the ±1.0ppm stability meets 3GPP requirements. I've specified this part in numerous cellular module designs with excellent results. The multi-voltage support (1.8V/2.8V/3.3V) is particularly useful for designs with multiple power domains. The price point is very competitive compared to Japanese alternatives, making it attractive for cost-sensitive cellular IoT products. What I appreciate most is the consistent supply and short lead times. For any LTE cellular design, this is my first-choice TCXO recommendation.
faeReview.highlight: Ideal for 4G LTE with multi-voltage support and competitive pricing.

alternativeParts.useCase: Carrier-grade LTE equipment requiring better stability

companionParts.description: Band 40 RF filter for LTE TDD applications
Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: What cellular standards does this TCXO support?
faqs.answer: The TCXO3225-38M4-10 with ±1.0ppm stability supports all major 4G LTE standards including FDD-LTE (Bands 1-32) and TDD-LTE (Bands 33-46). The 38.4MHz frequency is the standard reference for most LTE baseband processors from Qualcomm, MediaTek, and other vendors. The stability meets 3GPP requirements for cellular operation. This TCXO is also suitable for 3G WCDMA/HSPA applications. For 5G NR applications, the same frequency is used but may require even better stability depending on the specific 5G band and application. The multi-voltage support makes it compatible with various baseband processor I/O voltages.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for 4G LTE, 3G, and some 5G applications. Contact us for 5G-specific recommendations.

faqs.question: Can this TCXO be used at 1.8V?
faqs.answer: Yes, the TCXO3225-38M4-10 supports multiple voltage options including 1.8V, 2.8V, and 3.3V. When ordering, specify the required voltage as the part number suffix (e.g., -1V8 for 1.8V operation). The voltage selection affects the output logic levels - at 1.8V, the CMOS output swings 0V to 1.8V. Power consumption is slightly lower at 1.8V compared to 3.3V. The frequency stability and phase noise performance are maintained across all voltage options. This multi-voltage capability is valuable for designs with multiple power domains, such as smartphones where the baseband may use 1.8V I/O while other circuits use 3.3V.
faqs.decisionGuide: Specify voltage when ordering. Compatible with 1.8V, 2.8V, and 3.3V systems.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for TCXO3225-38M4-10?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of TCXO3225-38M4-10?
faqs.answer: The TCXO3225-38M4-10 has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for high-reliability applications. Contact us for detailed reliability reports.

faqs.question: What is the ESD sensitivity rating of TCXO3225-38M4-10?
faqs.answer: This component has an ESD HBM (Human Body Model) rating of 2kV and CDM (Charged Device Model) rating of 500V. While this provides reasonable protection, standard ESD precautions should always be followed during handling and assembly. Use grounded workstations, ESD-safe tools, and proper personnel grounding. Avoid touching pins directly. Store components in ESD-safe packaging until ready for use. For applications in high-ESD environments, additional external protection may be necessary. Contact our FAE team for ESD design recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard ESD precautions. Contact us for high-ESD environment recommendations.

categories.longDescription: Dapu Temperature Compensated Oscillators (TCXO) deliver exceptional frequency stability across temperature variations, making them ideal for GPS, cellular, and precision timing applications. These advanced oscillators use temperature compensation circuitry to achieve stability of ±0.5ppm to ±2.5ppm. As a Dapu distributor, we provide TCXO selection guidance for wireless communication, navigation systems, and industrial equipment. Dapu TCXOs offer GPS-grade performance, cellular compatibility, and competitive pricing for high-volume applications.

series.name: TCXO3225 Series
series.description: 3.2x2.5mm high-stability TCXOs for GPS and cellular

categories.name: Voltage Controlled Oscillators (VCXO)
categories.description: Precision VCXOs with wide pull range and low phase noise for PLL applications and frequency synthesis. Dapu VCXOs provide voltage-controlled frequency adjustment for synchronization and clock recovery applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select VCXOs
selectionGuide.description: Consider pull range, linearity, and phase noise when selecting VCXOs for PLL applications.

faqs.question: What is pull range in VCXOs?
faqs.answer: Pull range is the total frequency deviation achievable by varying the control voltage from minimum to maximum. It's specified in ppm (parts per million) relative to the center frequency. Common pull ranges include ±50ppm, ±100ppm, and ±150ppm. Wider pull range provides more frequency adjustment capability but typically results in poorer phase noise and stability. The pull range should be selected based on the expected frequency variation in the system plus margin. For PLL applications, the pull range must accommodate the VCO tuning range plus initial frequency tolerance. Dapu VCXOs offer various pull ranges to meet different application requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select pull range based on system frequency variation requirements. Contact us for PLL design assistance.

faqs.question: What is pull linearity and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Pull linearity measures how linearly the frequency changes with control voltage. It's typically specified as a percentage deviation from ideal linearity, with <10% being good and <5% being excellent. Good linearity is important for PLL applications because it affects loop stability and lock time. Non-linear VCXOs can cause loop instability or increased phase noise. Dapu VCXOs use high-quality varactor diodes and optimized circuit design to achieve excellent linearity. Linearity is measured across the full control voltage range and specified in the datasheet. For critical PLL applications, request linearity test data.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select VCXOs with <10% linearity for most PLLs, <5% for critical applications.

faqs.question: What is VCXO gain (Kvco) and why is it important?
faqs.answer: VCXO gain (Kvco) is the sensitivity of frequency to control voltage, measured in Hz/V or ppm/V. It's calculated as the frequency change divided by voltage change. Kvco is important for PLL design because it affects loop bandwidth, stability, and lock time. Higher Kvco means more frequency adjustment per volt but can make the PLL more sensitive to noise on the control line. Lower Kvco provides more stable operation but requires larger voltage swings for the same frequency range. Typical Kvco values range from 1kHz/V to 50kHz/V depending on pull range. When designing PLLs, use the actual Kvco from the datasheet for loop filter calculations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use higher Kvco for wide tracking range, lower Kvco for noise-sensitive applications. Check datasheet for exact value.

faqs.question: Can VCXOs be used in spread spectrum applications?
faqs.answer: Yes, VCXOs can be used for spread spectrum clock generation by modulating the control voltage with a triangle or random waveform. This spreads the clock energy over a wider frequency range, reducing EMI peaks. The pull range must be sufficient to achieve the desired spread percentage (typically ±0.5% to ±2%). Linearity is important for uniform spreading. The modulation frequency is typically 30-33kHz for FCC compliance. Dapu VCXOs with good linearity are suitable for spread spectrum applications. However, for dedicated spread spectrum applications, consider using spread spectrum oscillators (SSO) which integrate the modulation circuitry.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use VCXO with external modulation for spread spectrum. Consider dedicated SSO for simpler implementation.

faqs.question: What is the control voltage input impedance of Dapu VCXOs?
faqs.answer: Dapu VCXOs typically have high control voltage input impedance of >100kΩ, sometimes up to 1MΩ depending on the specific model. This high impedance minimizes loading on the control circuit (typically a DAC or op-amp output). The high input impedance means the VCXO control pin draws very little current, typically <1μA. This allows the use of high-impedance loop filters without significant voltage drop. The input is usually DC-coupled and may have internal protection diodes. Check the specific datasheet for exact input impedance values. The high impedance is maintained across the full control voltage range and temperature range.
faqs.decisionGuide: High input impedance >100kΩ. Compatible with high-impedance control circuits.

products.name: 25MHz VCXO ±100ppm Pull Range
products.shortDescription: 25MHz VCXO in 5.0x3.2mm package with ±100ppm pull range for PLL and synchronization applications.
descriptionParagraphs: The VCXO5032-25M-100 is a high-performance voltage-controlled crystal oscillator designed for PLL applications and frequency synchronization.
Featuring ±100ppm pull range, excellent linearity, and low phase noise, this VCXO provides reliable frequency adjustment for clock recovery and synchronization systems.
The 5.0x3.2mm ceramic package and 3.3V operation make it suitable for networking equipment and communication systems.
features: Wide ±100ppm pull range for PLL applications
Excellent linearity <10% for stable loop operation
Low phase noise for jitter-sensitive applications

faeReview.author: Kevin Zhao
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - PLL Applications
faeReview.content: The VCXO5032-25M-100 is my recommended VCXO for Ethernet clock recovery applications. The ±100ppm pull range provides adequate margin for tracking frequency variations while maintaining good phase noise. I've used this part in numerous Ethernet switch and router designs with excellent PLL stability. The linearity is consistently good across production lots, which is critical for PLL loop stability. The 5.0x3.2mm package is a good size for handling during assembly while not taking excessive board space. For customers designing synchronization equipment, this VCXO provides the right balance of pull range, linearity, and cost. I recommend it for any PLL application requiring 25MHz with moderate pull range.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent linearity and pull range for reliable PLL operation.

alternativeParts.reason: Narrower pull range for better phase noise
alternativeParts.useCase: Applications with smaller frequency variation range

alternativeParts.reason: Higher frequency for Gigabit Ethernet PLLs

companionParts.description: Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: How do I calculate the VCXO control voltage range?
faqs.answer: The VCXO control voltage range determines the frequency adjustment capability. For the VCXO5032-25M-100, the control voltage input range is 0.3V to 3.0V, providing the full ±100ppm pull range. The center frequency (0ppm deviation) typically occurs at VCC/2 (1.65V for 3.3V operation). The frequency vs. voltage characteristic is approximately linear, with sensitivity of about ±33ppm per volt. When designing the PLL control circuit, ensure the DAC or control amplifier can drive the required voltage range (0.3V to 3.0V). The control input impedance is typically high (>100kΩ), minimizing loading on the control circuit. For single-supply systems, the control voltage is referenced to ground.
faqs.decisionGuide: Ensure PLL control circuit can drive 0.3V-3.0V range. Center frequency at VCC/2.

faqs.question: What loop filter design is recommended for PLL using this VCXO?
faqs.answer: PLL loop filter design depends on the specific application requirements including loop bandwidth, phase noise, and lock time. For Ethernet clock recovery using the VCXO5032-25M-100, typical loop bandwidths range from 100Hz to 1kHz. A second-order passive loop filter is commonly used, with component values calculated based on the VCXO gain (Kvco), phase detector gain (Kpd), and desired bandwidth. The VCXO gain is approximately 3.3kHz/V (100ppm of 25MHz = 2.5kHz over 0.75V range). Dapu can provide application notes with recommended loop filter designs for common PLL configurations. For critical applications, we recommend loop stability analysis using PLL simulation tools.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard PLL design procedures with Kvco ≈ 3.3kHz/V. Contact us for application support.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for VCXO5032-25M-100?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of VCXO5032-25M-100?
faqs.answer: The VCXO5032-25M-100 has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for high-reliability applications. Contact us for detailed reliability reports.

faqs.answer: This component has an ESD HBM (Human Body Model) rating of 2kV and CDM (Charged Device Model) rating of 500V. The compact 3.2x2.5mm package is valuable for high-density telecom line cards where board space is at a premium. faqs.question: Why is 19.2MHz used in telecom applications?
faqs.answer: The 19.2MHz frequency is widely used in telecommunications because it's a common reference for various telecom standards. It's used in SDH/SONET equipment, GSM base stations, and various networking equipment. 19.2MHz can be easily multiplied to generate other common telecom frequencies like 155.52MHz (SONET/SDH), 125MHz (Gigabit Ethernet), and various cellular frequencies. The frequency is also compatible with many PLL chips designed for telecom applications. Dapu offers both fixed oscillators and VCXOs at 19.2MHz to support different telecom timing architectures. For new designs, verify the specific frequency requirements of your telecom chipset.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 19.2MHz for telecom applications. Verify specific chipset requirements.

faqs.question: What is the phase noise performance of this VCXO?
faqs.answer: The VCXO3225-19M2-50 offers good phase noise performance suitable for telecom applications. Typical phase noise is -125dBc/Hz at 1kHz offset and -145dBc/Hz at 100kHz offset. The phase noise is measured with the control voltage at center (VCC/2). Phase noise may degrade slightly at the edges of the pull range. For PLL applications, the integrated phase jitter (12kHz-20MHz) is typically <1ps RMS. Phase noise plots are available for qualification. For applications with stringent phase noise requirements, consider using a TCXO-VCXO combination or a low-noise fixed oscillator with a separate phase adjustment circuit.
faqs.decisionGuide: Phase noise suitable for most telecom applications. Contact us for phase noise plots.

Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. categories.name: RF Filters and Components
categories.description: High-performance RF filters and low-noise amplifiers for 5G, WiFi, Bluetooth, and cellular communication systems. Dapu RF components provide excellent selectivity and low insertion loss for wireless applications.

selectionGuide.title: How to Select RF Filters
selectionGuide.description: Consider center frequency, bandwidth, insertion loss, and rejection requirements when selecting RF filters.

faqs.question: What types of RF filters does Dapu offer?
faqs.answer: Dapu offers several types of RF filters for different applications. SAW (Surface Acoustic Wave) filters provide excellent selectivity for cellular and GPS applications from 400MHz to 2.7GHz. BAW (Bulk Acoustic Wave) filters offer higher Q and better performance for challenging interference environments. Ceramic filters are cost-effective solutions for ISM band and consumer applications. LC filters provide broadband performance for general-purpose filtering. Dapu also offers duplexers and multiplexers for FDD systems. Each filter type has different characteristics in terms of insertion loss, rejection, size, and cost. The choice depends on your specific frequency, performance, and cost requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select SAW for cellular, BAW for high performance, ceramic for cost-sensitive applications.

faqs.question: What is insertion loss and why does it matter?
faqs.answer: Insertion loss is the signal power loss through the filter, measured in dB. Lower insertion loss means more signal passes through the filter. Typical insertion loss ranges from 1-3dB for good filters, though narrowband filters may have higher loss. High insertion loss reduces receiver sensitivity and transmitter output power. In receive paths, every dB of insertion loss directly degrades system sensitivity. In transmit paths, higher insertion loss requires more PA power, reducing battery life. When selecting filters, balance insertion loss against other parameters like rejection and bandwidth. Dapu filters are optimized for low insertion loss while maintaining good rejection characteristics.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select filters with <2dB insertion loss for receive paths, <3dB for transmit paths.

faqs.question: What is the difference between SAW and BAW filters?
faqs.answer: SAW (Surface Acoustic Wave) filters use acoustic waves traveling along the surface of a piezoelectric substrate. They are cost-effective and work well up to about 2.7GHz. BAW (Bulk Acoustic Wave) filters use waves traveling through the bulk material, enabling higher Q factors and better performance at higher frequencies (up to 6GHz+). BAW filters have steeper roll-off, better temperature stability, and smaller size but cost more. For cellular and GPS applications below 2.7GHz, SAW filters are typically sufficient. For 5G high bands (3-6GHz) or applications requiring superior selectivity, BAW filters are preferred. Dapu primarily offers SAW filters for cost-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use SAW for cellular/GPS below 2.7GHz. Use BAW for 5G high bands or premium performance requirements.

faqs.question: How do I match RF filters in my circuit?
faqs.answer: Most Dapu RF filters are internally matched to 50Ω and don't require external matching components. Connect the filter using 50Ω transmission lines (microstrip, stripline, or coplanar waveguide). Keep traces short to minimize insertion loss and parasitic effects. Use ground vias near the filter ground connections. Avoid sharp bends in traces near the filter. The filter should be placed close to the antenna or LNA for receive paths, and close to the PA for transmit paths. For filters requiring external matching, the datasheet will provide the matching network schematic. Always follow the recommended PCB layout in the datasheet for best performance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use 50Ω transmission lines. Keep traces short. Follow datasheet layout recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the ESD rating of Dapu RF filters?
faqs.answer: Dapu RF filters typically have ESD ratings of 1-2kV HBM (Human Body Model) and 250-500V CDM (Charged Device Model). While this provides reasonable protection, standard ESD precautions should always be followed during handling and assembly. The SAW filter structure is sensitive to ESD damage which can degrade performance or cause failure. Use grounded workstations, ESD-safe tools, and proper personnel grounding when handling RF filters. Store components in ESD-safe packaging until ready for use. For high-ESD environments, consider adding external ESD protection devices. Contact us for specific ESD ratings for your filter part numbers.
faqs.decisionGuide: Standard ESD precautions required. 1-2kV HBM typical rating.

products.name: GPS SAW Filter 1575.42MHz
products.shortDescription: High-performance GPS L1 SAW filter in 1.6x0.8mm package with low insertion loss and excellent out-of-band rejection.
descriptionParagraphs: The RF1608-GPS is a high-performance SAW filter specifically designed for GPS L1 band applications at 1575.42MHz.
Featuring low insertion loss (<1.5dB), excellent out-of-band rejection (>40dB), and compact 1.6x0.8mm package, this filter provides excellent GPS signal selectivity.
The filter is ideal for GPS receivers, navigation devices, and location-based services requiring reliable GPS signal reception.
features: Low insertion loss <1.5dB for high sensitivity

faeReview.author: James Liu
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - RF Components
faeReview.content: The RF1608-GPS is an excellent GPS filter that delivers performance comparable to Japanese competitors at a much better price point. I've used this filter in numerous GPS tracker and wearable designs with excellent results. The insertion loss is consistently low, which is critical for GPS sensitivity. The rejection at cellular bands is excellent, preventing desensitization from nearby cellular transmitters. The 1.6x0.8mm package is tiny but still manageable for assembly. What impresses me is the consistency across temperature - some filters show significant degradation at temperature extremes, but this one stays stable. For any GPS L1 application, this filter provides excellent performance and value.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent GPS selectivity with low insertion loss in a tiny package.

alternativeParts.useCase: Applications where slightly larger package is acceptable

companionParts.description: GPS low-noise amplifier for receiver front-end
Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: Does this filter require external matching components?
faqs.answer: No, the RF1608-GPS is internally matched to 50Ω and does not require external matching components. This simplifies design and reduces BOM cost. The filter can be connected directly to the antenna or LNA with 50Ω transmission lines. The input and output are DC-blocked internally, so no external DC blocking capacitors are needed. The 50Ω impedance is maintained across the passband, ensuring good matching with standard RF components. For best performance, use controlled-impedance transmission lines (typically 50Ω microstrip or coplanar waveguide) to connect to the filter. Avoid long traces or vias near the filter to maintain signal integrity.
faqs.decisionGuide: No external matching required. Connect with 50Ω transmission lines for best performance.

faqs.question: What is the rejection at cellular bands?
faqs.answer: The RF1608-GPS provides excellent rejection at cellular bands to prevent interference from nearby cellular transmitters. Typical rejection is >40dB at 700-960MHz (LTE low band), >35dB at 1710-2170MHz (LTE mid band), and >30dB at 2300-2700MHz (LTE high band). This high rejection prevents the strong cellular signals from desensitizing the GPS receiver. The rejection is maintained across temperature and manufacturing variations. For applications with very high cellular interference, consider adding additional filtering or using a higher-rejection filter. The filter also provides good rejection at WiFi (2.4GHz) and Bluetooth frequencies.
faqs.decisionGuide: Excellent cellular rejection suitable for most GPS applications. Contact us for high-interference environments.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for RF1608-GPS?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of RF1608-GPS?
faqs.answer: The RF1608-GPS has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.

products.name: WiFi 2.4GHz SAW Filter

Featuring low insertion loss, excellent channel selectivity, and compact 2.0x1.2mm package, this filter provides reliable coexistence in crowded 2.4GHz spectrum.
The filter is ideal for wireless consumer electronics, IoT devices, and industrial wireless applications.

faeReview.author: Sarah Wang
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Wireless Components
faeReview.content: The RF2012-WIFI24 is a versatile filter for 2.4GHz applications. I've used it in WiFi modules, Bluetooth devices, and multi-protocol IoT designs. The full ISM band coverage means one filter works for WiFi, Bluetooth, and Zigbee, simplifying BOM management. The insertion loss is competitive, and the rejection at cellular bands helps with coexistence in smartphones. The 2.0x1.2mm package is a good balance of size and handling. What I like most is the consistent performance - no surprises in production. For any 2.4GHz wireless design, this filter provides reliable performance at a good price point.
faeReview.highlight: Full 2.4GHz band coverage ideal for multi-protocol wireless devices.

companionParts.description: Evaluation kit for testing and validation

faqs.question: Is this filter compatible with WiFi 6 (802.11ax)?
faqs.answer: Yes, the RF2012-WIFI24 is fully compatible with WiFi 6 (802.11ax) in the 2.4GHz band. WiFi 6 uses the same 2.4GHz frequency range (2412-2484MHz) as previous WiFi standards. The filter's 83MHz bandwidth covers the entire 2.4GHz WiFi band including all channels (1-14). The filter's performance characteristics (insertion loss, rejection) are adequate for WiFi 6 requirements. WiFi 6 does introduce new features like OFDMA and 1024-QAM, but these don't change the RF filter requirements significantly. For WiFi 6E (6GHz band), a different filter is required. This filter is also compatible with Bluetooth 5.0 and BLE.
faqs.decisionGuide: Compatible with all 2.4GHz WiFi standards including WiFi 6. Use different filter for 5GHz or 6GHz.

faqs.question: Can this filter be used for both TX and RX paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the RF2012-WIFI24 can be used in both transmit and receive paths. The filter is passive and bidirectional, providing the same insertion loss and rejection in either direction. In receive paths, the filter provides selectivity to reject out-of-band interference. In transmit paths, the filter attenuates harmonics and spurious emissions. The power handling is adequate for typical WiFi/BT transmit power levels (up to +20dBm). For higher power applications, verify power handling specifications. Using the same filter type for both TX and RX simplifies BOM and procurement. Some designs use separate filters for TX and RX in FDD systems, but for TDD systems like WiFi, one filter can be shared.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for both TX and RX in TDD systems. Verify power handling for high-power applications.

faqs.question: What is the recommended soldering profile for RF2012-WIFI24?
faqs.answer: Standard reflow soldering profile per JEDEC J-STD-020 is recommended. Peak temperature should not exceed 260°C for Pb-free solder. Preheat at 150-180°C for 60-90 seconds. Soak at 217°C for 60-90 seconds. Peak at 245-260°C for 20-40 seconds. Allow adequate cooling time. Hand soldering is possible with temperature-controlled irons at 350°C maximum, limited to 3-5 seconds per lead. Avoid thermal shock by not exceeding 4°C/second heating/cooling rates. Washable flux can be used but thorough cleaning is recommended. Follow IPC-A-610 for acceptance criteria.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard Pb-free reflow profile. Contact us for specific soldering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of RF2012-WIFI24?
faqs.answer: The RF2012-WIFI24 has an MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of >100 million hours at 25°C operating temperature, calculated per Telcordia SR-332. This corresponds to a FIT rate of <10 failures per billion device-hours. Reliability is verified through accelerated life testing at elevated temperatures. The high MTBF makes this component suitable for mission-critical applications. For specific reliability data including FIT rates at different temperatures, contact our technical support. Burn-in screening is available for high-reliability applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Suitable for high-reliability applications. Contact us for detailed reliability reports.

faqs.question: What is the ESD sensitivity rating of RF2012-WIFI24?
faqs.answer: This component has an ESD HBM (Human Body Model) rating of 2kV and CDM (Charged Device Model) rating of 500V. While this provides reasonable protection, standard ESD precautions should always be followed during handling and assembly. Use grounded workstations, ESD-safe tools, and proper personnel grounding. Avoid touching pins directly. Store components in ESD-safe packaging until ready for use. For applications in high-ESD environments, additional external protection may be necessary. Contact our FAE team for ESD design recommendations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use standard ESD precautions. Contact us for high-ESD environment recommendations.

categories.longDescription: Dapu RF Filters and Components provide high-performance signal conditioning for 5G, WiFi, Bluetooth, and cellular communication systems. These SAW and ceramic filters offer excellent selectivity, low insertion loss, and compact packages for wireless applications. As a Dapu distributor, we offer RF component selection guidance for GPS receivers, wireless modules, and IoT devices. Dapu RF filters deliver competitive performance for frequency bands including GPS L1, cellular bands, and 2.4GHz/5GHz WiFi.

series.description: 1.6x0.8mm ultra-compact SAW filters for GPS and wireless

series.name: RF2012 Series
series.description: 2.0x1.2mm SAW filters for WiFi and cellular applications
